title.en: Ensure root is the only GID 0 account

prose.en: Ensure root is the only GID 0 account

description.en: The `usermod` command can be used to specify which group the `root` account belongs to. This affects permissions of files that are created by the `root` account.

rationale.en: Using GID 0 for the `root` account helps prevent `root` \-owned files from accidentally becoming accessible to non\-privileged users.

audit.en:
Run the following command to set the `root` user's GID to `0`:  

Run the following command to set the `root` group's GID to `0`:  

Remove any users other than the `root` user with GID 0 or assign them a new GID if appropriate.

```
# groupmod -g 0 root
```

```
# usermod -g 0 root
```

remediation.en:
Run the following command to set the `root` user's GID to `0`:  

Run the following command to set the `root` group's GID to `0`:  

Remove any users other than the `root` user with GID 0 or assign them a new GID if appropriate.

```
# groupmod -g 0 root
```

```
# usermod -g 0 root
```

references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: CM-1, CM-2, CM-6, CM-7, IA-5
